brief_notes: Strong mechanistic paper challenging the prevailing model of noncanonical inflammasome assembly with clean experimental logic. Strengths: elegant construct design (WT, catalytic-dead, autoprocessing-deficient), orthogonal validation (microscopy + SEC + functional assays), clear feed-forward model, well-articulated revision of existing paradigm, and honest limitations section. The writing is among the best in this set -- precise and well-organized. Weaknesses: no figures, results presented as ranges rather than means with SD (e.g., 30-50%, 60-80%), uses iBMDMs with ectopic expression rather than endogenous replacement, no cryo-EM or crosslinking data to characterize intermediates, and the D285A mutant interpretation assumes no alternative processing sites without direct evidence. The paper would benefit from primary macrophage validation and structural characterization of the proposed dimeric intermediate.
